movies.title: In the Mood for Love
movies.note: Every frame is a painting. Wong Kar-wai understood longing better than anyone.

movies.title: Stalker
movies.note: The Zone is a mirror. Tarkovsky made patience feel transcendent.

movies.title: Spirited Away
movies.note: Pure imagination rendered with impossible care. Miyazaki's masterpiece.

movies.title: Mulholland Drive
movies.note: Lynch proved that confusion can be the most honest emotion.

movies.title: The Thing
movies.note: Paranoia as a group activity. Carpenter's practical effects still hold up.

movies.title: Aftersun
movies.note: Memory as a haunting. The camcorder footage wrecked me.

movies.title: Yi Yi
movies.note: Three hours of life itself. Edward Yang saw the poetry in the mundane.

movies.title: Blade Runner 2049
movies.note: Deakins turned every shot into a cathedral of light. The rare worthy sequel.

movies.title: Parasite
movies.note: Genre-fluid filmmaking at its finest. Bong Joon-ho is a trickster god.

movies.title: Paris, Texas
movies.note: The monologue scene through the one-way mirror is cinema at its most raw.

movies.title: Past Lives
movies.note: In-yun. The space between two people on a bench can hold an entire universe.

movies.title: 2001: A Space Odyssey
movies.note: Kubrick made the void feel sacred. Still the benchmark for cinematic ambition.

music.note: The sound of a band burning their playbook. Everything In Its Right Place rewired my brain.
Vulnerability as architecture. Every listen reveals a new room.
South London rain at 3am. The most evocative electronic album ever made.
Glaciers and beats colliding. Joga is a national anthem for a country that only exists in sound.
Recorded in a cabin with a single mic. You can hear the wind. Perfect.

music.album: Music Has the Right to Children
music.note: Nostalgia for a childhood that never happened. Warm analog melancholy.
The groove on Playa Playa is legally classified as a controlled substance.
Choosing joy after grief. Be Sweet is the best pop song of the decade.

books.title: Godel, Escher, Bach
books.author: Douglas Hofstadter
books.note: The book that made me see strange loops everywhere. Changed how I think about thinking.

books.title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
books.author: Haruki Murakami
books.note: Down the well, into the dream. Murakami's most labyrinthine and rewarding novel.

books.title: Seeing Like a State
books.author: James C. Scott
books.note: Legibility as a tool of power. Permanently changed how I view top-down design.

books.title: Invisible Cities
books.author: Italo Calvino
books.note: Every city is a poem. Calvino compressed infinite worlds into a few pages each.

books.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
books.author: Martin Kleppmann
books.note: The rare technical book that reads like a novel. My distributed systems bible.

books.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
books.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
books.note: Le Guin built a world to ask one question about gender, and the answer is frost and love.

interests.label: Typography
interests.description: Obsessed with how letterforms shape mood. Currently exploring variable fonts and kinetic type.

interests.label: Fermentation
interests.description: Making kimchi, kombucha, and sourdough. Controlled rot is the most rewarding hobby.

interests.label: Generative Art
interests.description: Writing code that makes beautiful things. Shaders, particle systems, L-systems, noise fields.

interests.label: Urban Exploration
interests.description: Finding hidden staircases, rooftops, and forgotten infrastructure in cities I visit.

interests.label: Mechanical Keyboards
interests.description: Deep in the thock rabbit hole. Current daily: a hand-lubed board with tactile switches.

interests.label: Long-Distance Running
interests.description: Trails over roads. The best ideas arrive somewhere around mile six.
